headlines.title: Former Trump adviser John Bolton to plead guilty to retaining national security information
headlines.description: Bolton described the national security information in an electronic diary entry he shared with two members of his family, sources familiar with the matter said.

headlines.title: Senate begins voting on Republican bill to fund ICE as GOP is split over Trump’s $1.8B fund
headlines.description: Some Republicans want to amend the bill to prohibit the Trump administration from setting up the “anti-weaponization” fund that could pay Jan. 6 rioters.

headlines.title: Trump says Bill Pulte will only serve in an acting capacity as DNI
headlines.description: President Donald Trump said Thursday that his newly appointed acting Director of National Intelligence, Bill Pulte, will not take up the position permanently

headlines.title: House committee calls on DOJ to investigate two men with ties to Jeffrey Epstein
headlines.description: The House Oversight Committee said Thursday it was asking the Justice Department to investigate sexual misconduct allegations made by Jeffrey Epstein’s former assistant, Sarah Kellen, against two of his associates

headlines.title: Cracks emerge in GOP support for Trump’s agenda: From the Politics Desk
headlines.description: In today’s edition, Jonathan Allen explores how congressional Republicans are starting to push back on some of President Donald Trump’s wishes.

headlines.title: Women who dated Graham Platner detail 'unsettling' behavior in NYT article
headlines.description: In a New York Times report, several women who dated Democratic Senate hopeful Graham Platner detailed "unsettling" behavior, describing volatile and "toxic" relationships. Platner has faced controversies on his campaign trail, and responded to the most recent report with a statement saying he has "been open about what was a very dark period" in his life.

headlines.title: House committee releases transcript of Bondi interview about Epstein files
headlines.description: The House Oversight Committee released the transcript of the interview with former Attorney General Pam Bondi regarding her handling of the Epstein files, during which she repeated that she delegated oversight of the review and investigation of the files to Todd Blanche. The committee also released the transcript of a prison guard working the night that Epstein died.

headlines.title: Ukraine's Amb. to U.S.: 'Every possible option' is on the table to defend against Russia
headlines.description: Ukraine's Ambassador to the U.S. Olga Stefanishyna joins Meet the Press NOW to discuss Ukrainian President Zelenskyy's public letter to Russian President Putin requesting direct peace talks after increased drone attacks between both countries.

headlines.title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio
headlines.description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

headlines.title: CIA puts senior officials on leave over officer arrested with gold bars
headlines.description: CIA officer David Rush is scheduled to appear in court Friday after the FBI said it found $40 million in gold bars stashed at his Virginia home.

headlines.title: Kennedy Center lawyers tell staff to remove references to Trump in signage
headlines.description: Attorneys for the iconic performing arts venue said the signage must revert to previous names like The John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts by June 12.

headlines.title: New York Democrats take first step toward drawing new congressional lines ahead of 2028
headlines.description: Years after handing redistricting power over to an independent commission, New York Democrats want it back as they join the national map-drawing battle with an eye on the 2028 election
